description: Baseline audit found one concrete security issue (Claude dangerous-mode prompt bypass), but full chezmoi apply validation timed out and needs a faster check path.

asi.insight: A single client-config default (`skipDangerousModePermissionPrompt`) was the clearest low-risk security win. Minimal docs/skill reinforcement helps prevent regressions even though the main metric was driven by the config fix.

asi.insight: After exact-command filtering, wc looked more like incidental implementation scope than an intentional repo workflow permission. The change follows the same pattern as dscl/tree: trim first, then add a drift warning.
asi.validation: Repo-local Claude settings JSON parses, health-check shell syntax passes, the existing run_after hot-path checks still pass, and targeted chezmoi dry-run validation remains green.
asi.best_practice: When a tracked permission only supports internal implementation details rather than documented workflows, prefer removing it and letting one-off needs use explicit approval instead of baking it into the default allowlist.

description: Baseline for repo-local Claude cat permission cleanup found one likely stale allow rule plus the lack of a matching health-check warning.

description: Remove Bash(cat:*) from the tracked repo-local Claude allowlist and add a matching health-check warning so file-reading defaults stay with dedicated tools instead of a broad shell-cat exception.

asi.hypothesis: Because this repo's agents are expected to inspect files with dedicated read tools, Bash(cat:*) is another reasonable permission to trim from the default Claude allowlist.
asi.insight: This call was less about raw repo usage counts and more about preferred workflow: the repo's agent harnesses already provide safer direct file-reading paths, so keeping cat in the default Bash allowlist was harder to justify than leaving it available via one-off approval.
asi.validation: Repo-local Claude settings JSON parses, health-check shell syntax passes, the existing run_after hot-path checks still pass, and targeted chezmoi dry-run validation remains green.
asi.best_practice: If an agent environment already offers a dedicated non-shell tool for a common task, keep the tracked shell allowlist narrow and rely on explicit approval for the shell fallback instead of pre-approving both paths.

description: Remove Bash(alias:*) from the tracked repo-local Claude allowlist and add a matching health-check warning so shell-alias convenience does not stay pre-approved without a documented workflow need.

asi.hypothesis: Because the repo documents aliases as shell config and prose rather than as agent workflow commands, Bash(alias:*) can be removed from the default Claude allowlist.

asi.validation: Repo-local Claude settings JSON parses, health-check shell syntax passes, the existing run_after hot-path checks still pass, and targeted chezmoi dry-run validation remains green.
asi.best_practice: Do not keep shell builtins or convenience affordances in a tracked default allowlist unless the repo actually documents a maintenance workflow that depends on them.

description: Remove the stale Influx WebFetch domain from the tracked repo-local Claude allowlist and add a matching health-check warning so unused explicit fetch domains do not silently return.

asi.hypothesis: Explicit WebFetch domains with no repo workflow footprint are low-risk cleanup candidates, just like stale Bash rules were.

asi.validation: Repo-local Claude settings JSON parses, health-check shell syntax passes, the existing run_after hot-path checks still pass, and targeted chezmoi dry-run validation remains green.
asi.best_practice: Treat explicit network domains like shell commands: if a tracked allowlisted endpoint no longer maps to a documented or implemented workflow, remove it and add a drift warning instead of assuming explicit means necessary.
